content:
- Staged the runner/doc/test/task changes and the new execution plan file.
- I intentionally left unrelated working tree noise unstaged: .DS_Store files and the unrelated .ralph/STOP deletion.
- Next step is a final staged diff sanity check, then commit with the full test evidence and push.

- Completed task 01-task-remove-runner-source-cockroach-access-and-config.
- Updated the task and plan records, ran Ralph task switch, committed the runner source-access removal, and pushed commit 5471754.
- All required lanes passed: make check, make lint, make test, make test-long.
- Left unrelated local noise untouched after push: .DS_Store files and the pre-existing .ralph/STOP deletion remain unstaged.

- Read prior Ralph progress entries and confirmed the last completed task was story-16 task 01 with all lanes green.
- Loaded the active and done task summaries; the only completed bug is already in done, so current selection is between unblocked failing non-bug tasks unless blocked-by metadata says otherwise.
- Next I am refreshing the Ralph task summary, reading choose-task rules, and inspecting task metadata plus repo surface to choose the right next task path.

- Refreshed Ralph summaries and checked active task files for blocker and priority tags.
- Confirmed there are no unblocked failing bug tasks left, no active priority-tagged tasks, and the only explicit active blocker is metrics task 02 blocked by metrics task 01.
- Inspected the actual repo architecture: root Rust workspace (`runner`, `ingest-contract`, `source-bootstrap`), a remaining vendored Go verify subtree, and README/tests still wired to bash/bootstrap-script flows.
- The most logical next task is story-16 task 03 because the runner already exposes no verify command in runtime code, while the repo still actively ships the legacy bash/source-bootstrap operator contract that this task is meant to remove.
- Next should be writing `.ralph/current_task.txt` to `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/03-task-remove-bash-bootstrap-flows-and-script-based-source-setup.md` and then quitting.

- Wrote the chosen next-task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Selected story-16 task 03 because it is an unblocked failing task and the current Rust workspace still contains the legacy `source-bootstrap` crate, README shell-script flow, and runner test harnesses built around rendered bootstrap scripts.
- After resuming, start that task with the tdd skill and validate `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, plus `make test-long` if the changes touch ignored long-lane coverage.

- Read Ralph progress log, current task pointer, and the requested `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill docs.
- Confirmed the active task path is `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/03-task-remove-bash-bootstrap-flows-and-script-based-source-setup.md`.
- Next I am reading that task file and its plan state to determine whether this turn is planning-only or execution.

- Read the task file. It is still `not_started` with no plan directory present, so per the Ralph instructions this turn should create a plan file before any execution.
- The task scope is removing bash/bootstrap script operator contracts and moving docs/tests toward SQL-output-only expectations.
- Next I am inspecting the current repo surface around `source-bootstrap`, docs, and existing story-16 plans so I can write a concrete plan with TDD slices and boundary cleanup targets.

- Inspected the remaining bash/script contract in `README.md`, `crates/source-bootstrap`, and the runner long-lane harnesses.
- Wrote the new task-03 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/03-task-remove-bash-bootstrap-flows-and-script-based-source-setup_plans/2026-04-19-sql-only-source-setup-plan.md`.
- The plan locks the next execution turn onto a SQL-only source-setup contract, with TDD slices for CLI rename, SQL-only rendering, README contract updates, and removal of script-based runner harness plumbing.

- Re-read the new task-03 plan and confirmed the interface decisions are concrete enough for execution.
- Flipped the plan footer from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next turn should read `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/03-task-remove-bash-bootstrap-flows-and-script-based-source-setup_plans/2026-04-19-sql-only-source-setup-plan.md`, follow the TDD slices exactly, and stop immediately if the SQL-only contract design proves wrong mid-execution.

- Read Ralph progress, current task pointer, active task file, and the SQL-only execution plan for story-16 task 03.
- Confirmed this turn is in NOW EXECUTE mode and that the repo still exposes the old shell-script contract in README, source-bootstrap tests/code, and runner long-lane harnesses.
- Next: inspect the concrete files for the first TDD slice, then add a failing CLI contract test for `render-bootstrap-sql` before changing implementation.

- Finished TDD slice 1: changed the source-bootstrap CLI contract test to require `render-bootstrap-sql` only, fixed the test import, got the expected red, then renamed the subcommand and neutralized the output type naming.
- Verified slice 1 with `cargo test -p source-bootstrap --test cli_contract` passing.
- Next: start slice 2 by replacing the bootstrap artifact test so it demands SQL-only output and explicitly rejects shell markers before rewriting the renderer.

- Finished TDD slice 2: replaced the main source-bootstrap artifact test with a SQL-only contract, got the expected red, and rewrote `crates/source-bootstrap/src/render.rs` to emit only SQL comments/statements instead of bash.
- Added database-qualified changefeed table references and removed dead shell helpers; also cleaned up one dead table helper method after seeing the compiler warning.
- Next: update README contract tests and README content so the published source setup instructions forbid `.sh` artifacts and describe emitted SQL only.

- Completed the SQL-only source-bootstrap refactor for story-16 task 03: CLI renamed to `render-bootstrap-sql`, renderer emits only SQL/comments, README switched to SQL artifact flow, and runner long-lane harnesses now render/apply SQL directly instead of writing/executing shell scripts.
- Required lanes are all green: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` (long lane passed, including the image test).
- Next: task bookkeeping only — task file/plan updated, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, inspect the resulting diff, commit all intended task files, push, and then stop immediately.
